raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section asserts a principal HCI contribution, an explicit gap (brand/capacity-cue confound), and a core causal claim; any review must reconstruct and evaluate whether these components are coherently stated.","R1.2":"A material internal contradiction is present: the paper asserts a fully blind protocol ('no model identifier,' 'routing log never surfaced') yet simultaneously describes a visible routing badge (green leaf / blue cloud) that participants hovered over before submitting ratings — these are logically incompatible, and the causal claim about content-driven trust rests entirely on the blind-protocol warrant.","R1.3":"Quantitative claims (71% attribution accuracy, 2.3× hover-dwell time) must be evaluated for inferential alignment with the blind-protocol framing; if participants could see routing badges, the attribution-survey baseline and hover-time interpretation are confounded.","R1.6":"'Review this section' canonically includes prose and paragraph quality; sentence-level clarity and narrative cohesion are expected outputs of any unqualified review request."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize scientific validity — logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment (making R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing) — or editorial prose quality (making R1.6 load-bearing)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a principal HCI contribution, an explicit gap (brand/capacity-cue confound), and a core causal claim; any review must reconstruct and evaluate whether these components are coherently stated.
rationale.R1.2: A material internal contradiction is present: the paper asserts a fully blind protocol ('no model identifier,' 'routing log never surfaced') yet simultaneously describes a visible routing badge (green leaf / blue cloud) that participants hovered over before submitting ratings — these are logically incompatible, and the causal claim about content-driven trust rests entirely on the blind-protocol warrant.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (71% attribution accuracy, 2.3× hover-dwell time) must be evaluated for inferential alignment with the blind-protocol framing; if participants could see routing badges, the attribution-survey baseline and hover-time interpretation are confounded.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' canonically includes prose and paragraph quality; sentence-level clarity and narrative cohesion are expected outputs of any unqualified review request.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize scientific validity — logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment (making R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing) — or editorial prose quality (making R1.6 load-bearing)?
